Lufthansa rejects union demands again
data.description:
FRANKFURT (dpa-AFX) - Lufthansa's airline boss, Jens Ritter, has once again rejected the Vereinigung Cockpit (VC) union's demands in the wage dispute as unrealistic. "We don't have anywhere near enough money to further improve our already very good company pension scheme," Ritter told Funke Mediengruppe. The union had previously given the airline more time to submit an improved offer and intends to refrain from industrial action for the time being.

Ritter said that Lufthansa already has one of the best pension schemes in the industry. He sees a possible compromise elsewhere: "Lufthansa crews are also much more interested in how the further shrinkage of the short-haul fleet can be stopped and prospects created." That is the real sticking point, and he could imagine concrete commitments being made here. "But to do that, the collective bargaining issues relating to retirement and transitional benefits must also be taken off the table."

Fewer domestic flights

Lufthansa had previously announced that it would be thinning out its domestic flight schedule for cost reasons. According to the summer flight schedule presented at the end of October, no domestic German routes will be discontinued, but there will be around 50 fewer flights per week to the Frankfurt and Munich hubs.

In the collective bargaining dispute, Cockpit is demanding higher employer contributions to the company pensions of some 4,800 pilots at Lufthansa's core company and Lufthansa Cargo. VC entered the negotiations with a demand for a tripling of the employer's contribution. However, no agreement was reached in the course of seven rounds of negotiations. At the end of September, VC members voted by a large majority in favor of strikes in a ballot./jcf/DP/zb

data.title: Ukraine says it has received 31 civilians who had been held in Belarus
data.description:
KYIV (Reuters) -Ukraine said it had received 31 civilians on Saturday who had been freed from jail in Belarus.

"Women and men detained in Belarus and sentenced to various terms of imprisonment ranging from two to 11 years are returning to Ukraine," Kyiv's prisoner exchange coordination committee said on the Telegram messaging app.

"We express our gratitude to the United States of America and President Donald Trump for their fruitful work in returning Ukrainian civilians and military personnel from Belarus and Russia," it added.

(Reporting by Anastasiia Malenko; Editing by Kevin Liffey)

Ukraine to meet US in Switzerland to discuss ending war
data.description:
KYIV (Reuters) -Kyiv will hold consultations in Switzerland with the U.S. on ending Russia's war on Ukraine, a top Ukrainian security official said on Saturday.

"...we are starting consultations between high-ranking officials of Ukraine and the United States on the possible parameters of a future peace agreement in Switzerland," Rustem Umerov, Secretary of the National Security and Defence Council, wrote on Telegram.

"We appreciate the participation of the American side and its readiness for substantive talks."

Umerov edited the post, without giving any reason, after a previous version said the talks would be held "with the participation of European partners".

President Volodymyr Zelenskiy has approved the composition of the delegation for talks, which will be led by the head of his office and includes top security officials, according to a statement on Telegram from his office.

He also approved instructions for the negotiations.

"Ukraine will never be an obstacle to peace, and representatives of the Ukrainian state will defend the legitimate interests of the Ukrainian people and the foundations of European security," the presidency statement said.

(Reporting by Anastasiia Malenko; Editing by Kevin Liffey)

Russia's Ryabkov says potential Putin-Trump summit is on the agenda
data.description:
MOSCOW (Reuters) -The possibility of another meeting between Russian President Vladimir Putin and U.S. President Donald Trump is on the agenda, a senior Russian diplomat has said.

"I wouldn't rule anything out," Deputy Foreign Minister Sergei Ryabkov said in an interview for the state-owned International Affairs magazine, published on Saturday.

"The search for a way forward continues," he said.

Ryabkov said contacts between Russia and the United States had not been suspended, and channels for dialogue continued to function.

Trump and Putin last met in Alaska in August, but failed to produce any agreement to resolve or pause Moscow's war in Ukraine. A subsequent plan to meet in Budapest was suspended indefinitely.

"We are working on an ongoing basis. We have well-established formats and channels. Not all of these channels are visible or audible, not all of them need to be discussed publicly, but the fact remains that everything is in working order."

Ryabkov said the progress in establishing dialogue between Russia and the U.S. was "impressive".

Ryabkov also commented on the possibility of a trilateral meeting with China and the United States on nuclear stability, saying that Moscow did not intend to push Beijing towards it.

"We have no questions for China on the subject of arms control and strategic stability," Ryabkov said, adding that Russia had not received any formal proposals from the U.S. regarding such a meeting.

Trump has expressed interest in getting China involved in nuclear arms reduction efforts alongside Russia and the U.S.

Last month, he said Putin had raised the prospect of a bilateral nuclear de-escalation, and China could be added to that effort.

(Reporting by Reuters; Editing by Kevin Liffey)

data.title: Any Ukraine peace plan must be accepted in Kyiv, says Polish president
EssilorLuxottica proposes taking stake of 5-10% in Armani, report says
data.description:
MILAN (Reuters) -Eyewear company EssilorLuxottica would be interested in taking a stake of between 5% and 10% in Armani but would not seek an active role in the management of the luxury fashion group, Italian business daily Il Sole 24 Ore reported on Saturday.

A restructure of the famed fashion house is expected following the death of founder and owner Giorgio Armani in September.

EssilorLuxottica was named in Armani's will alongside luxury conglomerate LVMH and L'Oreal as priority potential buyers of an initial stake of up to 15% in the company.

Citing unnamed sources, Il Sole said Franco-Italian company EssilorLuxottica had informed the Armani Foundation that it would be interested in becoming an investor but would seek a smaller stake and not ask for a seat on the board of Armani.

There was no immediate comment from Armani or EssilorLuxottica, whose brands include Ray-Ban.

(Reporting by Keith Weir and Elisa Anzolin;Writing by Keith Weir; Editing by Emelia Sithole-Matarise)

data.title: G20 summit declaration: We commit to working together under the United States presidency of G20 in 2026
Washington State resident dies in first confirmed H5N5 bird flu case, health department says
data.description:
(Reuters) -A Washington State resident receiving treatment for H5N5 avian influenza died on Friday, the state's health department said, in the first confirmed human case of this variant globally.

The deceased - of Grays Harbor County - was an older adult with underlying health conditions, the department said in a statement released late on Friday, adding the person owned a backyard flock of mixed domestic birds.

The department found the avian flu in the flock's environment, pointing to domestic poultry or wild birds as the likely source of exposure.

It said that the risk to the general public remains low, and no other individuals involved in the case have tested positive for avian influenza, commonly called bird flu.

"Public health officials will continue to monitor anyone who was in close contact with the patient for symptoms," the department said, adding: "There is no evidence of transmission of this virus between people."

Those who had exposure to the backyard flock and its environment were also being monitored for potential symptoms, the department said.

(Reporting by Preetika Parashuraman in Bengaluru; Editing by Emelia Sithole-Matarise)

Klingbeil Hopes for 'Prompt' Agreement on Combustion Engine Phase-Out
data.description:
BERLIN (dpa-AFX) - German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil is hoping for a unified position within the governing coalition on the phase-out of combustion engines by early December. "I want us to find a solution very soon," the SPD leader told the "Rheinische Post" (Saturday edition). His party, he said, had already made significant concessions in order to safeguard jobs in the automotive industry. When asked what he meant by "very soon," Klingbeil responded: "We should have a unified position as Germany in Brussels by early December."

Chancellor Friedrich Merz (CDU) intends to advocate for changes to the planned EU ban on the registration of new combustion-engine vehicles from 2035. However, the coalition has yet to reach a common stance on the issue. The Social Democrats generally want to stick to the 2035 phase-out date for new combustion engines. Recently, though, Klingbeil has shown openness to solutions that combine electromobility and combustion engine technologies.

The future belongs to electric cars, Klingbeil emphasized. On the path toward that future, Germany needs more technological options such as range extenders and plug-in hybrids. Above all, he said, unity is now essential. "We must present a unified front as Germany in Brussels," the Vice Chancellor stressed. If Germany improves the political framework, he added, automobile manufacturers must also make "an absolutely clear commitment to Germany as a location and to job security in this country."

Transport Minister Schnieder Calls for Reversal of Combustion Engine Ban

Federal Transport Minister Patrick Schnieder is also urging a swift agreement, but insists on reversing the EU's combustion engine ban. "Europeans are waiting for Germany's position. I believe the majority will follow us if we show more flexibility," the CDU politician told the "Neue Osnabrücker Zeitung."

While Schnieder also sees a clear trend "towards electromobility," he does not consider a full transformation of the industry to electric vehicles by 2035 feasible. "We cannot afford to insist on the 2035 combustion engine ban and potentially lose our economic foundation in the process," he told the newspaper. For him, a combustion engine ban is the wrong approach. "Combustion engines will still have a future all over the world," the minister said. "We are the country that has the technology, that is at the forefront, and we would be cutting ourselves off from all our expertise if we ban diesel and gasoline engines. We must remain open to all technologies."

data.title: Manufacturer and Dealer Registrations Inflate E-Car Numbers
data.description:
BONN/FLENSBURG (dpa-AFX) - An increasing number of electric vehicles (EVs) are being pushed onto the market through self-registrations by manufacturers and dealerships. In the first ten months of this year, nearly one in four newly registered battery electric vehicles (BEVs) fell into this category, according to figures from the Federal Motor Transport Authority (KBA). Two years ago, it was just one in six. This trend is also putting pressure on used car prices.

Thomas Peckruhn, president of the German Federation for Motor Trades and Repairs (ZDK), sees the rising self-registration figures as a "clear warning sign." The market, he says, is not currently being driven by "genuine customer demand," but rather "mainly by artificial impulses from manufacturers and dealers." If a quarter of new registrations are on their own books, it shows "how hesitant private and business customers actually are--especially when it comes to electric cars." As a result, the growth rates for electromobility are "strongly overstated."

More than 50 Percent Increase Compared to Two Years Ago

Specifically, there have been 102,520 BEV self-registrations so far this year. In the first 10 months of 2024, the figure was 67,895, compared to 70,313 in the same period in 2023--when the environmental bonus was still in effect, according to the KBA. The increase of more than 50 percent over the past two years is primarily due to automakers, who have increased their self-registrations by two and a half times. The share of electric vehicles among total car registrations this year stands at 18.4 percent, significantly higher than the 13.3 percent recorded in the same period in 2024 and just above the 18.0 percent in 2023, when the environmental bonus was still available.

Self-registrations are typically used by dealers and manufacturers to meet sales targets despite weak demand. These vehicles usually enter the market quickly as short-term registrations or as nearly new used cars with substantial price discounts. While this can be advantageous for many customers, it creates challenges for the industry, as these cars drive down prices in the used car segment.

Residual Values for Used EVs Have Plummeted

Used car prices for electric vehicles are already under pressure, as figures from market analyst DAT show. The so-called residual value, which indicates how much a car with typical mileage is worth three years after purchase compared to its original list price, averaged 48.8 percent for EVs in October. Two years ago, it was 58.1 percent. For combustion engine vehicles, residual values have declined much more slowly, and currently stand at 63 percent for gasoline cars and 61.3 percent for diesels--both considerably higher.

For new car customers, this can also be bad news: low residual values generally result in higher leasing rates. However, prices for new electric vehicles have also come under pressure recently. Regular discount analyses by industry expert Ferdinand Dudenhoffer show a marked increase in discount levels over the course of the year.

ZDK: High Electricity Costs and Lack of Infrastructure Are Obstacles

According to the ZDK president, the fact that electromobility has only partially caught on with customers so far is not due to price. "The main reason for sluggish demand for electric vehicles is not the high purchase price, but rather persistently high charging costs and the availability of charging infrastructure in residential areas," Peckruhn emphasizes. He also notes that the range of affordable small and mid-sized BEVs continues to grow steadily./ruc/DP/zb
